finding: PCS uses 5 parcours type icons: p1 (flat) through p5 (mountain summit finish)
notes: Confirmed from PCS profile-score-explained page and live race pages

finding: Overview page has stage list table under h4 Stages heading
notes: Verified on TdF 2024 and Paris-Nice 2024 overview pages

finding: ITT/TTT detected via stage name text containing (ITT) or (TTT)
notes: Regex: /\(ITT\)/i and /\(TTT\)/i on stage link text

finding: ITT parcours type is independent — ITTs can be p1 through p5
notes: TdF 2024 Stage 7 ITT=p1 and Stage 21 ITT=p4

finding: ProfileScore is an integer in sidebar under title ProfileScore
notes: Found on both stage result and classic result pages

finding: Classics have no stage list on overview page — profile on result page only
notes: Verified on Milano-Sanremo 2024 overview vs result page

finding: Classics maintain parcours profile year over year
notes: Domain knowledge — MSR always p2 and Lombardia always p4/p5
